pain_points.pain_point: Public expenditure related to the elderly will continue to increase.
pain_points.evidence: As the aging population continues to rise, public spending related to the elderly will also continuously increase, leading to structural deficits due to shrinking tax bases.

pain_points.pain_point: The shrinking working population affects tax revenue.
pain_points.evidence: As the labor force shrinks, the number of taxpayers correspondingly decreases, which exacerbates the financial situation.

pain_points.pain_point: Population aging will compress medium- to long-term economic growth.
pain_points.evidence: Population aging poses significant headwinds to medium- and long-term economic growth, making it difficult to find effective solutions to this issue.

pain_points.pain_point: Shortage of healthcare personnel and high rental costs negatively affect senior care quality.
